perturbation.original_text: 8 Recall. If Ingredients provided by Supplier under this Agreement are misbranded, contaminated, or otherwise unfit for human consumption at the time they are delivered to PNC or its TPM ("Defect"), PNC in its sole discretion will make a determination of the necessity of a recall, market withdrawal, inventory retrieval, or other action designed to prevent the distribution or sale of the affected Finished Products, plus the type, extent, method of handling, disposition of the Finished Products as well as any affected work in progress, and all other particulars involved in such an action (a "Recall"), and PNC will execute any Recall. Supplier, in its sole discretion, will make a determination of the necessity of a recall, market withdrawal, inventory retrieval or other action designed to prevent the distribution or sale of the Ingredients. Subject to Section 9.1, Supplier shall bear the complete responsibility for a Recall occasioned by a Defect in the Ingredient and shall indemnify PNC for [***] resulting from or related to the Recall. Any Recall occasioned by PNC labels or by tampering with the Ingredients after they have left Supplier's control, or by improper storing or handling by PNC, will not be considered a Defect.
perturbation.changed_text: 8 Recall. If Ingredients provided by Supplier under this Agreement are misbranded, contaminated, or otherwise unfit for human consumption at the time they are delivered to PNC or its TPM ("Defect"), PNC in its sole discretion will make a determination of the necessity of a recall, market withdrawal, inventory retrieval, or other action designed to prevent the distribution or sale of the affected Finished Products, plus the type, extent, method of handling, disposition of the Finished Products as well as any affected work in progress, and all other particulars involved in such an action (a "Recall"), and PNC will execute any Recall. Supplier, in its sole discretion, will make a determination of the necessity of a recall, market withdrawal, inventory retrieval or other action designed to prevent the distribution or sale of the Ingredients, except for cases involving misbranding, where PNC's determination shall prevail. Subject to Section 9.1, Supplier shall bear the complete responsibility for a Recall occasioned by a Defect in the Ingredient and shall indemnify PNC for [***] resulting from or related to the Recall. Any Recall occasioned by PNC labels or by tampering with the Ingredients after they have left Supplier's control, or by improper storing or handling by PNC, will not be considered a Defect.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause gives Supplier sole discretion in determining the necessity of a recall for Ingredients. The modified clause adds 'except for cases involving misbranding, where PNC's determination shall prevail.' This introduces a contradiction. While Supplier generally decides on recalls, PNC now has the final say in misbranding cases. This creates uncertainty about who ultimately decides on a recall, especially in situations where misbranding is suspected but not confirmed, leading to potential disputes and enforcement issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Ingredients provided by Supplier under this Agreement are misbranded, contaminated, or otherwise unfit for human consumption at the time they are delivered to PNC or its TPM ("Defect"), PNC in its sole discretion will make a determination of the necessity of a recall, market withdrawal, inventory retrieval, or other action designed to prevent the distribution or sale of the affected Finished Products, plus the type, extent, method of handling, disposition of the Finished Products as well as any affected work in progress, and all other particulars involved in such an action (a "Recall"), and PNC will execute any Recall. Supplier, in its sole discretion, will make a determination of the necessity of a recall, market withdrawal, inventory retrieval or other action designed to prevent the distribution or sale of the Ingredients, except for cases involving misbranding, where PNC's determination shall prevail. Subject to Section 9.1, Supplier shall bear the complete responsibility for a Recall occasioned by a Defect in the Ingredient and shall indemnify PNC for [***] resulting from or related to the Recall. Any Recall occasioned by PNC labels or by tampering with the Ingredients after they have left Supplier's control, or by improper storing or handling by PNC, will not be considered a Defect.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction by granting PNC the final say in recall decisions related to misbranding, while the original text and the contradicted section states that the Supplier has sole discretion in determining the necessity of a recall for Ingredients. This creates a direct conflict regarding decision-making authority for recalls, specifically in misbranding cases, which were previously under the Supplier's sole discretion. Now, two different sections of the contract grant different rights to different parties, triggering a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Neither Party will be liable for any breach of its obligations under this Agreement resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, an act of nature, drought, outbreak of foot and mouth disease, port and other transport strikes, war, fires, quarantine restrictions, insurrections or riots, energy shortages, embargo or the inability to obtain supplies or raw materials because of a global shortage or governmental action (a "Force Majeure Event"). Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, in the event of a Force Majeure Event, or any other circumstance that limits Fonterra's ability to produce or deliver product, Supplier will exercise its best efforts to comply with its obligations hereunder, mitigate the adverse impact on and not disfavor PNC, and will treat it in parity with its other customers.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Neither Party will be liable for any breach of its obligations under this Agreement resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, an act of nature, drought, outbreak of foot and mouth disease, port and other transport strikes, war, fires, quarantine restrictions, insurrections or riots, energy shortages, embargo or the inability to obtain supplies or raw materials because of a global shortage or governmental action (a "Force Majeure Event"). Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, in the event of a Force Majeure Event, or any other circumstance that limits Fonterra's ability to produce or deliver product, Supplier will exercise commercially reasonable efforts to comply with its obligations hereunder; however, Supplier shall have no obligation to treat PNC in parity with its other customers, and may prioritize fulfilling contracts with customers offering more favorable terms.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says that, even in a Force Majeure Event that limits Fonterra's ability to supply the product, it has to treat PNC in parity with other customers. The modified text says the opposite, it says Supplier will have 'no obligation to treat PNC in parity with its other customers' and can prioritize other contracts if the other customers are offering better terms. These clauses create a direct in-text contradiction regarding Supplier's obligation to PNC during a Force Majeure event, resulting in uncertainty about the level of commitment and fairness PNC can expect during such circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Party will be liable for any breach of its obligations under this Agreement resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, an act of nature, drought, outbreak of foot and mouth disease, port and other transport strikes, war, fires, quarantine restrictions, insurrections or riots, energy shortages, embargo or the inability to obtain supplies or raw materials because of a global shortage or governmental action (a "Force Majeure Event"). Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, in the event of a Force Majeure Event, or any other circumstance that limits Fonterra's ability to produce or deliver product, Supplier will exercise commercially reasonable efforts to comply with its obligations hereunder; however, Supplier shall have no obligation to treat PNC in parity with its other customers, and may prioritize fulfilling contracts with customers offering more favorable terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are identical to the changed text, indicating the modification occurred in the same location. The changed text directly contradicts the initial parity requirement by stating that the Supplier has 'no obligation to treat PNC in parity with its other customers and may prioritize fulfilling contracts with customers offering more favorable terms'. This introduces a clear in-text contradiction regarding the Supplier's obligations during a Force Majeure event. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
